title: Fastener including embedded sensor
abstract: A method for determining a load exerted on a fastener, a method of tightening a fastener with a fastening tool, a fastener, a fastening tool, and an electronic device. The fastener may include an antenna for receiving wirelessly transmitted power, a strain gauge, a wireless communication circuit, and a controller having an electronic processor and a memory. The method may include receiving, by the antenna, power transmitted wirelessly by an external device; generating, by the strain gauge, a signal indicative of the load exerted on the fastener; instructing, by the controller, the wireless communication circuit to transmit the signal indicative of the load; and transmitting, by the wireless communication circuit, the signal indicative of the load to the external device.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method of determining a load exerted on a fastener, the fastener including an antenna for receiving wirelessly transmitted power, a strain gauge, a wireless communication circuit, and a controller having an electronic processor and a memory, the method comprising: receiving, by the antenna, power transmitted wirelessly by an external device; generating, by the sensor, a signal indicative of the load exerted on the fastener; instructing, by the controller, the wireless communication circuit to transmit the signal indicative of the load; and transmitting, by the wireless communication circuit, the signal indicative of the load. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the fastener is a nut. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the external device includes a second wireless communication circuit, a power transmission circuit, and a second controller having a second electronic processor, and wherein the method further comprises, receiving, by the second wireless communication circuit, the signal indicative of the load. 4. The method of claim 3, further comprising, determining, by the second controller, the load exerted on the fastener based on the signal indicative of the load. 5. The method of claim 3, wherein the power transmission circuit includes an infrared transmitter. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the fastener further includes a printed circuit board (PCB). 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the PCB is received by slots formed within a surface of the fastener. 23 8. The method of claim 6, wherein the controller and the wireless communication circuit are mounted on the PCB. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein the sensor includes a strain gauge. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the strain gauge is mounted within a slot formed in a surface of the fastener. 11. The method of claim 1, wherein the external device is a smartphone. 12. A method of tightening a fastener with a fastening tool, the fastening tool including a drive element, a drive actuator for exerting a torque to the drive element, a power transmission circuit, a communication circuit, and a controller having an electronic processor, the method comprising: exerting, by the drive actuator, a torque on the drive element to tighten the fastener; transmitting, by the power transmission circuit, wireless power to the fastener; generating, by a sensor included in the fastener, a signal indicative of an amount of force applied to the fastener; receiving from the fastener, by the communication circuit, the signal indicative of the amount of force applied to the fastener; determining, by the controller, whether a target amount of force has been applied to the fastener based on the signal received from the fastener; and ceasing exertion of the force on the drive actuator in response to the target amount of force having been applied to the fastener. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein the fastener is a nut. 14. The method of claim 12, wherein the fastener includes an antenna for wirelessly receiving power from the power transmission circuit of the fastening tool. 15. The method of claim 12, wherein the sensor includes a strain gauge. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the strain gauge is mounted within a slot formed in a surface of the fastener. 17. The method of claim 12, further comprising, transmitting, by the communication circuit, a completion signal indicating the target amount of force applied to the fastener. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the nut further includes a second controller having a second electronic processor and a second memory, and a second communication circuit; and wherein the method further comprises, receiving, by the second communication circuit, the completion signal. 19. The method of claim 18, further comprising, storing, by the second controller, the target amount of force applied to the fastener in the second memory. 20. The method of claim 12, wherein the power transmission circuit includes an infrared transmitter. 21. The method of claim 12, further comprising, receiving, by a user-interface of the fastening tool, a value of the target amount of force. 22. The method of claim 12, wherein the nut includes a printed circuit board (PCB) received by slots formed within a surface of the fastener. 23. The method of claim 12, wherein the fastening tool includes a hydraulic torque wrench, the wrench including a drive actuator, and wherein the method further comprises exerting, by the drive actuator, a torque on the drive element to tighten the fastener. 24. The method of claim 23, further comprising, deactivating, by the controller, the drive actuator in response to the target amount of force having been applied on the fastener. 25. A fastener comprising: a body defining a slot; a printed circuit board (PCB) received by the slot; a sensor configured to measure a load exerted on the fastener; a wireless communication circuit; and a controller having an electronic processor and a memory, the controller being supported by the PCB and configured to receive a signal indicative of the load exerted on the fastener from the sensor, and instruct the wireless communication circuit to transmit to an external device the signal indicative of the load exerted on the fastener. 26. The fastener of claim 25, wherein the sensor includes a strain gauge. 27. The fastener of claim 25, wherein the fastener further includes a power supply circuit configured to supply power to the controller and the wireless communication circuit. 28. The fastener of claim 27, wherein the power supply circuit includes an antenna configured to wirelessly receive power from an external energy source. 29. The fastener of claim 28, wherein the external energy source includes a smartphone. 30. The fastener of claim 28, wherein the external energy source includes a fastening tool. 31. The fastener of claim 27, wherein the power supply circuit includes an internal energy storage device. 32. A fastening tool comprising: a power transmission circuit; a wireless communication circuit; and 26 

 a controller including an electronic processor and a memory, the controller being configured to transmit, by the power transmission circuit, wireless power to a fastener, receive from the fastener, by the communication circuit, a signal indicative of an amount of force applied to the fastener, and determine a force exerted on the fastener based on the signal. 33. The fastening tool of claim 32, further comprising: a drive element; and a drive actuator for exerting a torque to the drive element to tighten the fastener. 34. The fastening tool of claim 33, wherein the controller is further configured to determine whether a target amount of force has been applied to the fastener based on the signal received from the fastener, and cease exertion of torque on the drive actuator in response to the target amount of force having been applied to the fastener. 35. The fastening tool of claim 34, further comprising a user-interface configured to receive the target amount of force as an input. 36. The fastening tool of claim 32, wherein the controller is further configured to store the amount of force applied to the fastener in the memory. 37. The fastening tool of claim 32, wherein the power transmission circuit includes an infrared transmitter. 38. An electronic device comprising: a user-interface; a power transmission circuit; a wireless communication circuit; and 27 

 a controller including an electronic processor and a memory, the controller being configured to transmit, by the power transmission circuit, wireless power to a fastener, receive from the fastener, by the wireless communication circuit, a signal indicative of a load exerted on the fastener, determine the load exerted on the fastener based on the signal, and display, by the user-interface, the load exerted on the fastener. 39. The electronic device of claim 38, wherein the controller is further configured to transmit to the fastener, by the wireless communication circuit, a second signal requesting information associated with the load on the fastener. 40. The electronic device of claim 38, wherein the controller is further configured to store, in the memory, a record of the load exerted on the fastener over time. 41. The electronic device of claim 38, wherein the power transmission circuit includes an infrared transmitter. 42. The electronic device of claim 38, wherein the electronic device is one selected from a group consisting of a smartphone, a handheld scanner, a tablet, and a laptop. 28
